title: Hangrix host repo agents.yml
description: Schema for the TEAM-LEVEL .hangrix/agents.yml. Mirrors the parse contract enforced by internal/agentsconfig. Roles are NOT declared here — each role is a separate file .hangrix/agents/<role-key>.md whose YAML front matter follows the #/$defs/agentFrontMatter shape below (the Markdown body is the role's prompt). Unknown keys are silently dropped for forward compatibility; malformed value types and out-of-range values are still rejected.

version.description: Schema version. Only 1 is currently accepted.

tools.description: Reusable tool rules: map of rule-name to a list of platform tool-name glob patterns (`*` wildcard). A role references rule names in its `tools:` front-matter field and sees the union of their patterns. Whitelist-only and platform-tools-only — local tools (read/write/edit/glob/grep/bash/webfetch) are never restricted. Enforced agent-side (hidden from the LLM tool schema); the server gates the v1 API by `permission:` alone.

container.description: Runtime environment shared by every role. Exactly one of `image` or `build` must be set.

image.description: Fully-qualified registry pull spec, e.g. ghcr.io/acme/dev:1.2.3.

dockerfile.description: Repo-relative path to the Dockerfile; conventionally .hangrix/agent.Dockerfile.

context.description: Build context root, repo-relative. Defaults to repo root when omitted.

args.description: Build-arg map passed to `docker build --build-arg`. String values only.

entrypoint.description: Overrides the container's PID 1. First element becomes `docker create --entrypoint`; subsequent elements are appended after the image name as CMD args. Omit / empty list = the runner uses its built-in default `/usr/bin/sleep infinity` so the container is a passive docker-exec sandbox. Set to e.g. `["/init"]` (s6-overlay) when the image bakes in a supervisor that should auto-start background services.

env.description: Plain-text env vars injected into every role container. Keys uppercase `[A-Z_][A-Z0-9_]*`; values support `${VAR_NAME}` whole-value references to repo variables and secrets (expanded by the runner at session start).

volumes.description: Repo-scoped named caches bind-mounted into the container.

name.description: Platform-scoped identifier (e.g. pnpm-store). Runner namespaces by repo id automatically.

mount.description: Absolute path inside the container. `..` segments are rejected.

llm.description: LLM tuning block. Appears at team level (top-level llm:) and per-role. Merge is field-by-field: any field present at role level overrides team, any field omitted at role level inherits team's value. At team level `model` is required; at role level `model` MAY be omitted to inherit team's. (JSON-Schema cannot express that conditional, so `required` is not declared here — the Go parser enforces it.)

model.description: Model name; must appear in some provider's allowed_models at runtime. Required at team level; optional at role level (omit to inherit team).

reasoning_effort.description: Thinking intensity. The parser accepts any string for forward-compat with new upstream enum values; canonical values 'minimal' / 'low' / 'medium' / 'high' drive the Anthropic adapter's thinking.budget_tokens translation (minimal/low → 1024, medium → 4096, high → 16384, temperature dropped) and pass through verbatim on OpenAI-compat adapters. Other non-empty strings are forwarded verbatim — the upstream decides whether to honour or reject. Empty string is equivalent to omission.

max_context_tokens.description: Agent runtime cap on prompt + conversation-history tokens before truncation. 0 = no cap declared here.

max_output_tokens.description: Per-call output budget (Anthropic max_tokens, OpenAI max_output_tokens). 0 = let the upstream apply its default.

temperature.description: OpenAI-style sampling temperature. Dropped by the Anthropic adapter when thinking is enabled.

agentFrontMatter.description: Shape of the YAML front matter in a `.hangrix/agents/<role-key>.md` file (the role-key is the filename). The Markdown body after the front matter is the role's prompt and is required (it is not a front-matter field). This `$def` is documentation: the Go parser validates each agent file, not agents.yml, against this shape.

triggers.description: Event subscription map (GitHub Actions style). Keys are recognised trigger constants; values are the per-event filter block (empty mapping `{}` = no filter). No wildcards.

permission.description: GitHub-style repo permission level, enforced server-side on the platform v1 REST API. `read` allows read-only endpoints (GET issue/comment/todo/contribution/mergeability/sessions); `write` additionally allows every mutation (comment, edit, merge, close, review, todo update, contribution apply/close/set_meta, release ops, attachment upload, session recover, issue create). Omitted defaults to `read` (fail-safe).

tools.description: List of tool-rule names (from the team-level agents.yml `tools:` map) this role references. The role's platform-tool visibility is the union of the named rules' glob patterns; everything else is hidden from the LLM tool schema (agent-side). Local tools are never restricted. Omitted = no platform tools. The fine-grained capability knob on top of the coarse `permission` level.

mcp.description: MCP server whitelist. Each element names a server key from the repo-root .mcp.json. When absent or empty the role loads no MCP servers; when non-empty only the listed servers are loaded. Referencing a non-existent server causes the agent session to explicitly fail at startup.

scope.description: Soft path-glob hint injected into the role's prompt; not enforced by pre-receive.

triggerFilterless.description: Filter block for triggers that take no parameters. Must be null or an empty mapping; any property is a misconfiguration.

issues.description: Issue behaviour configuration. When omitted every switch uses its default (recommended).

delete_branch_on_merge.description: When true, the issue branch is automatically deleted after a successful merge. Set false to keep merged branches for post-hoc inspection.

reviewers.description: Per-contribution review gate. A contribution branch (issue-<N>/<role>/<slug>) is matched against `rules` by its changed paths: it collects the union of reviewers from every rule whose globs match any changed file. When no rule matches, `fallback` reviews it. The required reviewers determine when the contribution leaves `pending` (every required reviewer has voted) and whether it becomes `approved` (all approve/abstain) or `rejected` (any reject). Omit this block to run no review gate. Reviewer wake-up is a separate concern, driven by each role's `commit.pushed` trigger.

rules.description: Ordered path-to-reviewers mappings. A contribution unions the reviewers of every rule it matches.

paths.description: Doublestar globs (GitHub Actions semantics) matched against a contribution's changed files. The rule matches if at least one changed file matches at least one pattern.

reviewers.description: Role keys that must review a contribution this rule matches. Each must name a role that exists AND can cast review votes (has issue_review_vote in its tool ACL). The Go parser enforces both cross-references; JSON Schema cannot.

fallback.description: Reviewer role(s) used only when no rule matches a contribution's changed paths (conventionally the maintainer). Required and non-empty whenever a `reviewers:` block is present, so a contribution can never be left with no possible reviewer. Same role-exists + can-vote constraints as rule reviewers.

triggerCommentFilter.description: Filter block for `issue.comment`. All fields default to no-filter; AND-combined when set. Unknown keys are silently dropped, so a typo (e.g. `mention_only:` for `mentioned_only:`) parses cleanly but leaves the filter empty — diff against the canonical keys below if a trigger fires more or less often than expected.

mentioned_only.description: When true, the role fires only if its own key is in the comment's @agent-<key> mention list.

from_roles.description: Fires only for comments posted by an agent whose role key is in this list.

from_users.description: Fires only for comments posted by a human user whose platform login is in this list.

triggerPushFilter.description: Filter block for `commit.pushed` and `patch.submitted`. AND-combined when both lists are set. Glob semantics mirror GitHub Actions / doublestar. For `patch.submitted` the globs match against the patch's changed_paths rather than git commit diffs. Unknown keys are silently dropped — see `triggerCommentFilter` for the same forward-compat note.

paths.description: Include globs. Push wakes the role only if at least one changed file matches at least one pattern.

paths_ignore.description: Ignore globs. Push wakes the role only if at least one changed file is NOT covered by these patterns (every-file-ignored push is skipped).
